# PYCOM: Python DICOM Processing Toolkit for BEAMnrc/DOSXYZnrc.

PYCOM is a collection of DICOM processing tools coded in the Python programming 
language for assisting MC simulation using BEAMnrc/DOSXYZnrc using RT plans 
generated from a TPS system. It currently has tools: 

* pycom.py: generating input files automatically for BEAMnrc/DOSXYZnrc 
from DICOM RT Plans (DICOM RP) exported from a Treatment Planning System (TPS). 

* pycom_dose.py: converting the dose distribution generated by DOSXYZnrc 
in 3ddose format to DICOM RT Dose (DICOM RD) format so that it can be imported 
by a DICOM processing software.

* pycom_anon.py: anonymizing DICOM files for patient privacy. 

See comments in the files for the usage of the tools.

_Note: The current version was developed based on BEAMnrc version 2011_

---

This version was developed under Python2. Here are the steps for setting up a Python2 environment to run this package:

1. Install a latest Linux OS with Python2.7 included, such as Ubuntu 22.04 or RHEL7/8. We use Ubuntu 22.04 as the example here.

2. Set up GIT

```
sudo apt install git
sudo apt install gh
gh auth login
gh repo clone https://github.com/lixinzhan/pycom.git
```

2. Run commands below to set up the Python2.7 virtual environment

```
sudo apt install python2
sudo apt install python2-pip
sudo pip2 install virtualenv
virtualenv pycom/venv2
```

3. Set up the PYCOM running environment

```
cd pycom
source rc.py2env
pip2 install -r requirements.txt
```
